learningObjective: Understand that integrals measure area under a curve - students place a numerical prediction for area, then watch the region fill to reveal the true value, building intuition that curved shapes have precise measurable areas
ahaMoment: Realizing that narrow-but-tall sections contribute as much area as wide-but-short sections, and that systematic over/underestimation patterns emerge based on curve concavity
targetMisconception: Students think area only applies to simple geometric shapes

miniSpec.title: Predict-Then-Measure Area Betting
miniSpec.narrative_wrapper: The student sees a curved region on the dashboard's route elevation profile and places a numerical prediction token showing their guess for the area. When they press 'Measure', the region fills incrementally with animated liquid (like a fuel tank filling), and a running counter shows the true area climbing toward the final value. Their prediction token remains frozen on screen, letting them see how far off they were.
miniSpec.manipulated_variable: The predicted numerical area value itself, typed or dialed before revelation
miniSpec.primary_visual_feedback: The prediction token locks in place; then liquid fill animation begins from bottom-up, with a live running counter that climbs smoothly from zero to the true area value, showing the gap between prediction and reality
miniSpec.goal_structure: Commit-a-prediction (place numerical guess), then reveal (trigger fill animation to see true area); repeat with new curve or new bounds to calibrate intuition
miniSpec.causal_chain_exposed: Running area counter during fill shows rate of area accumulation; fill speed visually correlates to curve height at each horizontal position, revealing that taller sections contribute more area per unit width
miniSpec.trace_strategy: Previous prediction tokens remain as faint ghosts with error bars, building a history of improving estimates across multiple trials
miniSpec.aha_moment: Realizing that narrow-but-tall sections contribute as much area as wide-but-short sections, and that systematic over/underestimation patterns emerge based on curve concavity

interactionContract.student_changes: drags the left and right bounds (a, b) along the x-axis
interactionContract.student_sees: the shaded region grows/shrinks and the area value updates in real-time

modelVariables.areaIntuition_boundA: left bound of integration region, student-dragged along x-axis
modelVariables.areaIntuition_boundB: right bound of integration region, student-dragged along x-axis
modelVariables.areaIntuition_trueArea: derived: the true computed area under the curve between bounds a and b
modelVariables.areaIntuition_fillProgress: derived: 0-1 progress of the fill animation during reveal
modelVariables.areaIntuition_animatedArea: derived: the current displayed area value during animation (climbs from 0 to trueArea)
modelVariables.areaIntuition_revealed: boolean: whether the reveal animation has been triggered
modelVariables.areaIntuition_samplesUsed: number: how many fuel token samples the student has clicked
modelVariables.areaIntuition_samplePoints: array: x-positions where the student has sampled
modelVariables.areaIntuition_estimatedDistance: derived: the Riemann sum estimate based on sample points

assertions.description: On load, the shaded region is visible but area is not revealed - posing the challenge of estimating area under a curve
assertions.visualCheck: shaded region visible between bounds, no fill animation running, area value hidden or showing placeholder

assertions.description: The left bound (a) can be dragged along the x-axis
assertions.visualCheck: left bound handle moves to x=2, shaded region shrinks from left

assertions.description: The right bound (b) can be dragged along the x-axis
assertions.visualCheck: right bound handle moves to x=4, shaded region shrinks from right

assertions.description: Dragging bounds causes the shaded region and area value to update in real-time
assertions.visualCheck: shaded region expands to cover more area under curve, area readout increases

assertions.description: Clicking on the timeline to sample speed costs one fuel token
assertions.visualCheck: a sample marker appears at the clicked x-position, fuel token counter decreases by 1

invariants.description: The left bound is always less than or equal to the right bound
The computed area is always non-negative
